FitValue.description: Describes the semantic fit between a Fide entity type and an external standard.

Statement.description: A record of a subject-property-object assertion.
Statement.litmus: Not a verified fact; verification is application-level. Not what it represents.

ruleDetails.rule: Reference MUST use `subjectFideId|propertyFideId|objectFideId`, where the middle slot MUST reference a Property entity, in that order.

DirectionalProperty.description: A property where the ordered pair (subject, object) is semantically significant.
DirectionalProperty.litmus: Use when reversing subject and object changes the meaning and produces a different Statement identity.

SymmetricProperty.description: A property where subject-object order is semantically interchangeable. Reversing subject and object preserves meaning and MUST resolve to the same canonical Statement identity.
SymmetricProperty.litmus: Use when `A -> p -> B` means the same thing as `B -> p -> A`, such as identity or mutual-peer relations.

Person.description: A person (alive, dead, undead, or fictional).

Organization.description: A durable collective of people, software agents, or both that can act as a unit.
Organization.litmus: Not a single person or software agent. Legal incorporation is not required, but a legal company is always an Organization. A branded initiative, product line, or program is usually a Concept unless it has its own durable collective agency. Not a query-defined group.

SoftwareAgent.description: A running process that acts as an independent actor: bot, AI agent, smart contract. Can hold keys and sign.
SoftwareAgent.litmus: Not a person or organization. Not static passive code (use CreativeWork for repos, images, logic).

NetworkResource.description: A resource identified primarily by addressability or resolution, such as a URL, URI, or CID.
NetworkResource.litmus: Not a subset or account. Not an inhabitable spatial context (use Place).

requirements: Reference MUST use an absolute URI in compliance with [RFC 3986](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986).

ruleDetails.rule: `http(s)` references MUST use a non-local hostname and normalized form before hashing, including lowercase protocol and hostname and no default ports, in compliance with the [URL Standard](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/).
`did` references MUST use `did:<method>:<method-specific-id>` in compliance with [W3C DID](https://www.w3.org/TR/did-core/).
`urn` references MUST use `urn:<nid>:<nss>` in compliance with [RFC 8141](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8141).
`acct` references MUST use `acct:user@host` in compliance with [RFC 7565](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7565).
`mailto` references MUST use `mailto:user@example.com` in compliance with [RFC 6068](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6068).
References SHOULD use identifiers that are not expected to change over time when such identifiers are available.

PlatformAccount.description: An authority-based account principal (e.g., an email address or a specific GitHub, Google, or Mastodon user ID). The identity exists by the permission of a host system's database.
PlatformAccount.litmus: Authenticated by a host authority's database. This represents the account principal itself, not the agent or agents that operate it.

CryptographicAccount.description: A math-based account principal (e.g., an EVM address, Bitcoin wallet, or did:key). The identity exists mathematically and is authenticated via mathematical signature keys.
CryptographicAccount.litmus: Authenticated via mathematical signature keys, independent of a host authority.

CreativeWork.description: The most generic kind of creative work, including books, movies, photographs, software programs, etc. Non-expressive digital artifacts (binaries, model weights, dataset snapshots) are CreativeWork with refinement via Statements (artifactKind, mediaType, generatedBy, derivedFrom).
CreativeWork.litmus: Not a raw value or formal concept. Not a running agent instance (use SoftwareAgent). Not a resource where location is primary identifier (use NetworkResource).

Concept.description: A socially or formally recognized unit of meaning, such as a term, topic, category, label, initiative, or other abstract idea.
Concept.litmus: Not a plain raw value. Use this for labels, brands, programs, or initiatives that do not have their own durable collective agency.

Place.description: A spatial context that can contain presence, co-location, and interaction. Includes physical locations and persistent virtual environments (e.g., a concert venue, a Discord server or channel, a Zoom room, a VRChat instance). Physicality/virtuality is asserted via Statements, not the base type.
Place.litmus: Not a happening or a physical artifact. Can an Actor have presence there? Is its identity a spatial address rather than a document location or account?

Event.description: An occurrence or happening bounded in time. Can be observed/aggregated; may be caused by Actions, systems, or nature. Occurs at a Place (physical or virtual).
Event.litmus: Not a specific exertion of agency (Action).

Action.description: A discrete assertion or commitment attributable to an Agent. Often produces or records a change (e.g., a transaction, API call, signature). Can be linked to Events via Statements (causes, records, resultsIn).
Action.litmus: Not a broad temporal happening (Event); must be driven by an Agent.

PhysicalObject.description: A tangible, inanimate physical object (e.g., hardware, vehicle, physical document, natural object). Physical only. Use Statements (madeBy, manufactured=true) if man-made vs natural matters.
PhysicalObject.litmus: Not a location (where). Not a digital artifact (use CreativeWork).

TextLiteral.description: A plain text sequence of characters.

requirements: Reference MUST use the exact text value.
Reference MUST use a base-10 integer string ([XML Schema 1.1 Part 2: Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema11-2/#integer)).

ruleDetails.rule: Integer references MUST NOT use decimal points, scientific notation, or thousands separators.

requirements: Reference MUST use a base-10 decimal string ([XML Schema 1.1 Part 2: Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema11-2/#decimal)).

ruleDetails.rule: Decimal references MUST NOT use scientific notation or thousands separators.

requirements: Reference MUST be `true` or `false` ([XML Schema 1.1 Part 2: Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema11-2/#boolean)).

ruleDetails.rule: Boolean references MUST use lowercase text values.

DateLiteral.description: A calendar date in canonical `YYYY-MM-DD` form.

requirements: Reference MUST use canonical `YYYY-MM-DD` format without timezone ([XML Schema 1.1 Part 2: Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema11-2/#date)).

ruleDetails.rule: Date references MUST use a four-digit year and zero-padded month and day values.

YearMonthLiteral.description: A calendar year and month in canonical `YYYY-MM` form.
YearMonthLiteral.litmus: Not a full date or year alone.

requirements: Reference MUST use canonical `YYYY-MM` format without timezone ([XML Schema 1.1 Part 2: Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema11-2/#gYearMonth)).

ruleDetails.rule: Year-month references MUST use a four-digit year and zero-padded month.

YearLiteral.description: A calendar year in canonical `YYYY` form.
YearLiteral.litmus: Not a year-month or full date.

requirements: Reference MUST use canonical `YYYY` format without timezone ([XML Schema 1.1 Part 2: Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema11-2/#gYear)).

ruleDetails.rule: Year references MUST use a four-digit year value.

TimeLiteral.description: A time of day (formatted according to ISO 8601, represented in UTC).

requirements: Reference MUST use UTC time format with a trailing `Z` ([XML Schema 1.1 Part 2: Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema11-2/#time)).

ruleDetails.rule: Time references MUST use zero-padded hour, minute, and second values.

DateTimeLiteral.description: A specific point in time in canonical UTC `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ` form.
DateTimeLiteral.litmus: Not a date or time alone.

requirements: Reference MUST use canonical UTC datetime format with a trailing `Z` ([XML Schema 1.1 Part 2: Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema11-2/#dateTime)).

DurationLiteral.description: A calculated amount of elapsed time (ISO 8601 duration format).

requirements: Reference MUST use ISO 8601 duration format ([XML Schema 1.1 Part 2: Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema11-2/#duration)).

ruleDetails.rule: Duration references MUST begin with `P` and use valid duration components.

URILiteral.description: A formalized Uniform Resource Identifier string.

ruleDetails.rule: Reference Type `URILiteral` is only valid when the Entity Type is also `URILiteral`.
Reference MUST be a valid URI string in compliance with [RFC 3986](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986).

JSONLiteral.description: A structured JSON object represented in canonical string form.

requirements: Reference MUST use canonical JSON string form ([RFC 8785](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8785)).

ruleDetails.rule: JSON references MUST be serialized in a stable canonical form before hashing.
